evals.prompt: Create a test strategy for our e-commerce platform. It's a Next.js frontend calling a Node.js API which uses PostgreSQL. We have 5 developers and 1 QA engineer. Key features: product search, cart, checkout (Stripe), user accounts.
evals.expected_output: A comprehensive test strategy document covering: test pyramid choice (unit-heavy given team size), specific scope per test level, tooling recommendations (Vitest for unit/integration, Playwright for E2E), coverage targets, critical E2E flows (checkout, auth), team responsibilities, and quality gates.
expectations: Document defines test levels (unit, integration, E2E) with scope for each
Document specifies coverage targets (numbers, not just 'good coverage')
Document identifies critical E2E flows (checkout at minimum)
Document specifies tooling choices with rationale
Document defines quality gates (what blocks merge vs what blocks deploy)
Document addresses team responsibilities (who writes what)

evals.prompt: What should our testing strategy be for a set of Python microservices that communicate via REST APIs? We have an auth service, a user service, and an orders service. Each has its own database. We need to test them both in isolation and together.
evals.expected_output: A test strategy addressing: unit tests per service (mocking other services), API contract tests between services (Pact), integration tests with real DBs, end-to-end service integration tests, and recommendations on when to use TestContainers vs mocks.
expectations: Document covers unit testing each service in isolation
Document addresses API contract testing between services
Document covers integration testing with real databases (TestContainers mentioned or similar)
Document addresses how to test cross-service flows
Document provides tooling recommendations for Python microservices
Document discusses mock vs real service in tests
